other_observers: many other observers over the course of the winter.; I first observed the bird 10/26/2012. Mark Chavez took photos on 10/27/2012.
specific_location: North 39 deg. 39' 49.6" West 105 deg. 12' 11.7" North 39 deg. 39.826' West - 105deg. 12.196'
description: Fairly large sparrow with lemon yellow forehead bordered by heavy black borders. On top of the head and hind neck the yellow becomes light gray; lores and ear coverts both gray; nape brownish gray. Bill is dark with the upper mandible darker than the lower mandible. The iris is brown. Back and scapulars broadly streaked with brownish-black. Rump is grayish brown with long,brown, slightly notched tail. The wings are brown with chestnut brown on the primaries and two narrow white wingbars. The throat, side of neck and breast are gray with buffy tones on the sides and flanks. The feet are pale pinkish brown.Behaviors: The Golden-crowned Sparrow came to the feeders behind the Red Rocks Trading Post. The sparrow would come out from the dense brush and undergrowth to feed on the ground in a tiered garden like setting with two large apple trees providing a canopy covering.Call: did not singPlumage: non-breeding plumage
